one_line_thesis: Serial-acquirer industrial compounder with record FCF and backlog; premium multiple caps IRR to 5.78% at spot → watch.

deep_dive.executive_summary: AMETEK is a high-quality industrial compounder built around two segments: Electronic Instruments and Electromechanical. The AMETEK Growth Model combines operational excellence, tuck-in and platform acquisitions, and new product development. FY2025 posted record sales ($7.40 billion), orders, backlog ($3.58 billion), and free cash flow ($7.23 per diluted share). Q1 2026 accelerated with 11% revenue growth (roughly 5% organic, 4% from deals, 2% foreign exchange) after FARO and LKC closings. Segment margins sit in the mid-20s to high-20s with strong cash conversion. At ~$233, Lawrence synthesi...

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

timeline.label: Board approved 10% quarterly dividend increase to $0.34 from $0.31 per share
Closed LKC Technologies acquisition (ophthalmic diagnostics); joins Electronic Instruments Group
FY2025 record results: $7.40B sales, $1.67B free cash flow, $3.58B backlog
Closed FARO Technologies acquisition ($933M 2025 deal spend with Kern); 3D measurement platform
Acquired Kern Microtechnik (precision machining and optical inspection)
Paragon Medical acquisition integration; medical components scale in EMG
Completed 15 acquisitions through 2025 (~$1.8B annualized sales) under AMETEK Growth Model
David A. Zapico continues as Chairman and CEO; long-tenured leadership on Growth Model execution

industry.structure: Fragmented niche markets for electronic test instruments, process controls, and precision electromechanical components. AMETEK competes on specification, reliability, and integration capability rather than commodity price. Top positions in targeted sub-niches with mid-20s to high-20s operating margins.
industry.share_shift: Scale serial acquirers (AMETEK, Danaher, Roper, IDEX) continue consolidating small engineered-product businesses. AMETEK gained share via FARO, LKC, and Paragon medical deals while organic growth ran mid-single digits.
industry.trend: Aerospace/defense and medical demand resilient; industrial automation cyclical. Acquisition multiples remain competitive; operational excellence and global sourcing offset inflation. Backlog at record levels supports near-term revenue visibility.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Premium multiple compression if acquisition pace slows or industrial demand softens while the market still prices perfection.
Integration risk on larger deals (FARO scale) could linger in margins longer than guided.
End-market cyclicality in aerospace and automation could cut organic growth below mid-single digits.
